text: Experts’ consensus on key competences in education for sustainable development (ESD) is still fairly broad, particularly at the higher education level, although it has not yet been empirically validated and based on students and graduates’ opinions. The competences required should be promoted at ESD study programs, which is a central research question for this paper. Within the framework of the evaluation of the University of Bern study programs in sustainable development, students (N=124), graduates (N=121), and their internship supervisors (N=37) took part in a structured questionnaire. In addition to answering a number of predefined questions, they also assessed the importance of acquiring the 13 competences addressed by this study, within the context of their studies as well as their professional work. In general, all respondents rated these key competences as being highly relevant.
